primary_reason: This narrative review summarizes global syphilis trends, disparities, and surveillance gaps without developing or evaluating a reusable surveillance or analytic method.
The study develops and cross-validates a dengue forecasting framework combining evolutionary sampling, missing-data imputation, parameter optimization, and random forests.
The study uses pre-versus-post vaccine serotype surveillance to estimate direct and indirect PCV10 effects, serotype replacement, and underreporting.
The study compares registered HIV transmission modes with questionnaire, risk-factor, and HCV-based classifications to diagnose surveillance misclassification.
This small retrospective study describes gastrointestinal symptoms in a neurological syndrome and does not provide a concrete infectious-disease method transfer route.
This single histoplasmosis case illustrates an unusual clinical presentation without contributing a transferable epidemiologic or surveillance design.
The review concerns a noninfectious transfusion complication and supplies no concrete route for transferring its content to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The review compares dengue rapid tests and the tourniquet test while assessing included-study quality with STARD and QUADAS-2.
The pilot implements and evaluates a smartphone notification workflow for improving serious AEFI reporting across teaching hospitals.
The review constructs and applies a staged risk-management framework to China's changing mpox preparedness, emergency response, and routine surveillance.
The review challenges the United States' nonendemic Chagas classification by synthesizing evidence of vectors, reservoirs, and autochthonous human transmission.
The review addresses artificial-intelligence diagnosis of immunodeficiencies rather than an infectious outcome and does not specify a concrete epidemiologic transfer route.
The study compares medical-record evidence with official death certification to quantify underreporting of HIV and AIDS as underlying causes of death.
The article offers general recommendations for Indian STI surveillance without enough methodological detail to evaluate a surveillance design or correction procedure.
The study descriptively tabulates tuberculosis cases and patient characteristics from DATASUS without a reusable adjustment or evaluation design.
The qualitative feasibility study measures suicide risk among people with HIV but does not establish a concrete infectious-disease epidemiology transfer route.
The literature review summarizes reported strongyloidiasis prevalence and diagnostic limitations in Brazil without comparing or prescribing epidemiologic methods.
The study describes severe RSV cases in national surveillance data but does not implement a method for correcting the acknowledged diagnostic underascertainment.
The review summarizes the epidemiology, outcomes, and management of MBL-producing Enterobacterales without a transferable analytic or surveillance contribution.
The study uses time-series regression to infer undocumented community varicella calls and adjust burden estimates for pandemic-related irregularities.
The review surveys Enterococcus gallinarum biology, resistance, diagnostics, and therapies without substantively evaluating a reusable epidemiologic method.
The systematic review compares deterministic, stochastic, and agent-based infectious-disease models and analyzes their data-quality and implementation limitations in underserved settings.
The database study primarily evaluates dehydration and electrolyte prescribing across mixed noninfectious conditions without a concrete infectious-disease method contribution.
The narrative review summarizes Guaroa virus epidemiology, clinical features, and research gaps without evaluating a transferable method.
The review describes systemic endemic mycoses and broad surveillance needs without developing or comparing a surveillance or analytic approach.

The pharmacovigilance perspective compares spontaneous reporting and cohort event monitoring during COVID-19 and gives concrete preparedness recommendations for automation, linkage, and signal detection.
The study combines a weighted vulnerability index with geospatial mapping to identify district-level maternal STI screening priorities.
The qualitative study identifies stigma-driven underreporting of weight loss that can reduce the sensitivity of WHO tuberculosis symptom screening.
The study applies a locally derived virological correction factor to national hospital data to estimate underreported RSV incidence and costs.
The study combines delay deconvolution, an underreporting multiplier, EpiEstim, and sensitivity analysis to evaluate regional COVID-19 transmission and interventions.
The study uses a generalized additive mixed time-series model with pathogen-circulation data to infer RSV-attributable hospitalizations missing from administrative codes.
This colorectal-cancer review does not study an infectious outcome or provide a concrete route into infectious-disease epidemiologic methods.
The review assesses diagnostic, surveillance, and control limitations for tick-borne diseases and prescribes an integrated One Health surveillance strategy.
The historical article recounts documented measles epidemics and social determinants without a reusable analytic or measurement design.
The study combines paired serology, regression mixture classification, and Bayesian estimation to measure typhoidal Salmonella infection incidence beyond clinical surveillance.
The systematic review synthesizes laboratory-acquired infection cases by pathogen, exposure risk, and biosafety practice to identify surveillance and prevention gaps.
The multidisciplinary registry concerns noninfectious sudden cardiac death and offers no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study validates an explainable XGBoost model for provincial leptospirosis outbreak risk across pre-pandemic, pandemic, and post-pandemic periods.
The study reports parasite prevalence and molecular species identification in sampled rats without a transferable surveillance evaluation or adjustment method.
The cross-sectional study reports leptospirosis seroprevalence and associated characteristics using standard tests and regression without a reusable design contribution.
The study uses a structured veterinary-expert workshop to identify and rank system-level barriers to African swine fever control.
The retrospective case series concerns penile cancer, and HPV status is a risk factor rather than a concrete infectious-surveillance or transmission outcome.
The study combines GBD estimates with Bayesian age-period-cohort projections, decomposition, and frontier benchmarking to characterize rabies burden and performance gaps.
The study integrates tuberculosis incidence data, lineage genotyping, and rpoB sequencing to conduct molecular surveillance of transmission and resistance emergence.
The review critically compares culture, serological, and molecular approaches for diagnosing brucellosis and identifies their surveillance consequences.
The study demonstrates whole-genome and Mash-based correction of conventional Klebsiella species misidentification while characterizing resistance genes.
A modified Delphi process produces explicit Asian recommendations for adult RSV case definitions, testing, surveillance integration, underestimation adjustment, and vaccination.
The narrative synthesis describes Nigerian Zika seroprevalence and diagnostic gaps without substantively comparing or developing methods.
The study applies four disproportionality algorithms and sensitivity analyses to detect infection and other safety signals in two pharmacovigilance databases.
The study introduces exposure density to standardize occupational infection-exposure risk across a changing workforce and identifies experience-stratified patterns.
The ecological HIV study combines regression, ARIMA, comparability testing, and spatial analysis to examine cross-national incidence trends and border disparities.
This retained-surgical-blade case concerns noninfectious surgical safety and supplies no concrete infectious-disease method transfer route.
The mixed-methods study prospectively solicits adolescent vaccine adverse events and perceptions to supplement potentially incomplete passive AEFI reporting.
The GBD analysis combines cause-of-death ensembles, seroprevalence synthesis, migration adjustment, and Bayesian compartmental modeling to estimate Chagas burden.
The protocol specifies multisource capture-recapture and geolocation methods for estimating leishmaniasis underreporting from laboratory, hospital, and regional records.
The study demonstrates wastewater RT-qPCR surveillance for underreported enteroviruses and explores seasonal co-detection of multiple viral targets.
The study combines field validation, spatial zero-inflated models, and random forests to measure horse bridge-host density and Hendra spillover risk.
The brief review describes bloodborne-pathogen hazards in dentistry and general reporting needs without enough methodological content for a reusable guidance role.
The study formulates and validates an age-stratified SIR model comparing constant and time-varying case-detection assumptions for influenza forecasting.
The review applies random-effects proportional meta-analysis and reporting-quality assessment to synthesize norovirus prevalence and genotype evidence in Ghana.
The theoretical study models how underreporting and ascertainment delays asymmetrically constrain intervention timing under threshold and feedback-control decision rules.
The study uses repeated community symptom measurements, multiple imputation, longitudinal k-means, and hierarchical aggregation to derive long-COVID trajectories.
The study uses branching-process simulations to train and test a graph neural network that infers superspreading from early spatial invasion patterns.
The situation report summarizes recent African avian-influenza outbreaks and surveillance gaps without evaluating a transferable method.
The prospective cohort uses repeated serology and illness-triggered PCR to measure interepidemic dengue and chikungunya seroconversion and associated risks.
The linked cohort compares two vaccination data systems to quantify agreement, underreporting, provider differences, and timing of pregnancy immunization.
The narrative review broadly summarizes parasitic infections, vaccines, and One Health needs without developing or comparing an epidemiologic method.
The dual-phase study combines vaccine stock-use analysis with stakeholder interviews to diagnose monitoring failures across public and private sectors.
The systematic review assesses communicable-disease surveillance designs, digital platforms, case definitions, and environmental integration across LMICs and prescribes system improvements.
The study compares survey-reported AEFI notifications with official counts to quantify reporting discrepancies and identify reasons for nonreporting.
This letter extrapolates another study's SMS findings to the Philippines without presenting an evaluated method or original implementation evidence.
The study compares centralized and decentralized wastewater sampling against reported influenza cases to detect local transmission, seasonal onset, and reporting delay.
The study implements flight-level wastewater PCR, whole-genome sequencing, and lineage analysis as a noninvasive surveillance system for imported SARS-CoV-2 variants.
The scoping review charts mpox surveillance structures, diagnostics, reporting strategies, and implementation barriers to derive multilevel surveillance recommendations.
The study develops a Melioidosis Checklist Index from clinical, exposure, demographic, and spatial evidence to support early diagnosis and case reporting.
The two-cycle quality-improvement audit evaluates education, equipment, and reporting-culture interventions for preventing and managing bloodborne-pathogen exposures.
The study develops and validates a multicriteria spatial risk-mapping approach for African swine fever in domestic pigs, wild boars, and their interface.
The review concerns hereditary skin-cancer risk and provides no concrete infectious-disease epidemiology transfer route.
The surveillance report descriptively compares acute hepatitis B cases in Korean and foreign nationals without a reusable correction or evaluation method.
The linkage study concerns maternal mortality as a whole and does not establish a concrete infectious-disease surveillance transfer route.
The study applies a PRISMA review, quality appraisal, and random-effects meta-analysis to synthesize confirmed Oropouche clinical and epidemiological evidence.
The study uses a nonlinear distributed-lag model, thresholds, variance decomposition, and impulse responses to estimate avian-influenza outbreak effects on egg markets.
The scoping review and case report describe infection-associated encephalopathy features and geographic occurrence without a transferable epidemiologic method.
The variant-specific Mendelian model predicts hereditary cancer risk and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study validates self-reported adult vaccination against linked electronic and immunization records using sensitivity, specificity, and net bias.
The study combines interviews, observations, group model building, and causal-loop diagrams to identify feedback mechanisms obstructing Kenyan rabies policy.
The national database study concerns breast reconstruction access and costs without a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The review summarizes hepatitis E epidemiology and general One Health priorities without substantively evaluating or prescribing a method.
The study combines whole-genome sequencing, lineage assignment, mutation analysis, and phylogenetics for regional SARS-CoV-2 transmission surveillance.
The machine-learning study predicts high-risk alcohol use rather than an infection, transmission, or infectious-surveillance outcome.
The study adjusts representative survey prevalence for excluded populations and behavioral underreporting to create denominators for HIV and hepatitis C monitoring.
The disease primer summarizes Buruli ulcer pathogenesis, diagnosis, treatment, and research needs without an epidemiologic methods contribution.
The study descriptively profiles reported occupational biological exposures without evaluating a reporting correction or transferable analytic design.
The hospital-discharge analysis concerns noninfectious rectus-diastasis surgery and lacks a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study applies zero-inflated negative-binomial models to animal-disease reports to forecast outbreaks and guide One Health surveillance investment.
The database analysis concerns muscle wasting and burn outcomes without a concrete infectious-disease epidemiology transfer route.
The linked retrospective cohort uses time-varying Cox models to estimate real-world COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness against death.
The systematic review compares healthcare-associated-infection surveillance tools, study designs, laboratory methods, and standardization gaps across Africa.
The paper defines a surveillance-oriented category for complex chronic AEFIs and proposes longitudinal reporting, patient-reported outcomes, transparency, and analytic reforms.
The retrospective review describes reported needlestick injuries by time and department without evaluating a reporting or prevention method.
The study uses community-level wastewater biomarkers and machine learning to predict COVID-19 prevalence and assess case underreporting after testing mandates ended.
The study compares local and departmental counts for a heterogeneous set of men's health conditions but contributes no reusable infectious-disease design.
The case demonstrates molecular and proteomic confirmation of seronegative brucellosis and identifies a specific deficiency in Mexico's surveillance case definition.
The survey concerns inpatient illicit-drug injection and care disruption without measuring an infectious outcome or specifying an infectious epidemiology transfer route.
The study relates wastewater RNA to testing-adjusted community cases and prospectively relevant hospital-acquired SARS-CoV-2 incidence thresholds.
The anthrax application combines semi-synthetic data, sliding-window fitting, and outbreak simulations to compare buffalo vaccination strategies under reporting delays.
The review applies confirmation criteria to ceftriaxone treatment-failure reports and identifies inconsistent definitions and diagnostic access as causes of underdetection.
The study combines archival records and quantified epidemiological data to reconstruct measles mortality, seasonality, and historical intervention responses.
The bibliometric review evaluates qualitative research across medical specialties rather than an infectious-disease outcome and offers no concrete epidemiologic transfer route.
The study combines expert-elicited foodborne and underreporting multipliers with direct and indirect costing to estimate the true economic burden of foodborne disease.
The targeted review compares numerical multipliers and methods used to adjust COVID-19 economic models for underreported infections, hospitalizations, and deaths.
The systematic review uses random-effects pooling, subgroup analysis, sensitivity analysis, and bias appraisal to synthesize transfusion-transmitted infections in Cameroon.
The study combines Bayesian smoothing, spatial autocorrelation, hotspot detection, scan statistics, seasonality tests, and trend regression to map Brazilian foodborne outbreaks.
The study links passive and sentinel surveillance through Bayesian multipliers and bottom-up costing to estimate underreported dengue cases and economic burden.
The systematic review pools vaccine seroconversion and safety evidence with subgroup, leave-one-out, and bootstrap analyses for solid-organ transplant recipients.
The review concerns cardiovascular toxicity from decongestants, and infectious respiratory indications do not supply a concrete infectious-outcome method contribution.
The article identifies seven operational drivers of African mpox transmission and prescribes locally aligned surveillance, laboratory, data, communication, and vaccination reforms.
The study develops a scalable multisource method integrating wastewater and seroprevalence data to estimate variant-specific shedding, infections, and transmission dynamics.
The time-series implementation combines active and passive case finding, WHO clinical criteria, and rapid tests to strengthen primary-care leptospirosis surveillance.
This textiloma case concerns a noninfectious postoperative foreign-body reaction and provides no infectious-disease epidemiology transfer route.
The review summarizes Chapare virus biology, clinical features, and preparedness gaps without developing or comparing an epidemiologic method.
The study develops and analyzes a dynamic-contact compartmental model with an evolving susceptible network, extinction threshold, and numerical epidemic simulations.
The article uses case studies and assessment data to evaluate a regional infodemic-management alliance and extract transferable coordination and capacity-building lessons.
The state-of-the-art review summarizes mpox clades, diagnostics, treatments, vaccines, and surveillance gaps without substantively evaluating a method.
The study combines multisource meta-analysis with Bayesian joint spatial modeling to map linked human and poultry salmonellosis risk and reporting anomalies.
The study audits Brazil's prion surveillance records against expected incidence and documents underreporting, database mismatch, and unsuitable data infrastructure.
The literature review synthesizes recurring causes of operating-room needlestick underreporting and prescribes simpler reporting and stronger safety culture.
The narrative review summarizes climate-sensitive waterborne-disease evidence and broad surveillance priorities without comparing or developing methods.
The study combines surveillance-data auditing with stakeholder interviews to evaluate Senegal's mpox system and readiness for interoperable AI-supported epidemic intelligence.
The narrative review compares proposed explanations for African COVID-19 outcomes and vaccine heterogeneity without contributing a reusable epidemiologic method.
The article recounts Somalia's diphtheria crisis and advocates general responses without enough analytic or guidance methodology for inclusion.
The protocol specifies three linked prospective cohorts, expanded molecular testing, and surveillance comparisons to estimate community intestinal-disease incidence and underreporting.
This single post-dengue transverse-myelitis case provides clinical observations without a transferable epidemiologic, diagnostic-validation, or surveillance design.
The quasi-experimental quality-improvement study evaluates tailored consent, planning, and tracking tools using linked vaccination-register coverage before and after implementation.
The study uses long-term hospital-record review, period stratification, spatial concentration, and notification auditing to monitor endemic leishmaniasis.
The ecological time-series study estimates chikungunya-associated excess mortality with two complementary baselines and compares estimates with routine surveillance deaths.
The review summarizes Japanese encephalitis epidemiology, diagnosis, and vaccines without developing or substantively comparing an epidemiologic method.
The large phenome-wide study integrates high-resolution HLA genotypes with underreported phenotypes and replicates associations including infectious susceptibility.
The study applies boosted regression trees to ecological and socioeconomic covariates to model global tick-borne-virus reporting probability and surveillance inequity.
The article substantively explains an integrated biophysical approach using smoothed epidemic curves, corrected testing, fatality ratios, and reproduction-number estimation.
The decade-separated surveys compare blood-exposure reporting behavior and use regression to identify predictors of exposure and failure to report.
The study primarily describes all-cause sickness absenteeism during the pandemic and provides no concrete infectious-disease epidemiology transfer route.
The interrupted time-series study concerns mental-disorder compensation claims, and pandemic timing alone does not create an infectious-disease outcome or transfer route.
The review integrates epidemiological, clinical, genomic, spatial, and national-surveillance evidence to reconstruct long-term chikungunya reemergence in Bangladesh.
The study combines self-report with a blood alcohol biomarker to measure exposure underreporting and its association with HIV viral nonsuppression.
The nationwide serosurvey uses ELISA screening, confirmatory agglutination, and cross-reactivity testing to measure otherwise underdiagnosed tularemia exposure.
The review describes molecular mechanisms and genetics of post-kala-azar dermal leishmaniasis but does not contribute a transferable epidemiological method.
The study descriptively compares vaccine-preventable disease incidence by age, region, and year without a transferable surveillance adjustment or evaluation design.
The study applies adjusted reporting-odds comparisons and onset-time analysis to generate antibiotic safety signals among lower-respiratory-infection reports.
The study embeds pharmacist review and staff training into antibiotic pharmacovigilance to improve adverse-reaction detection, reporting, and antimicrobial management.
